releases.title: v0.2.1
releases.summary: First checkout-backed JUnit readiness validation evidence with Spring Petclinic REST and AssertJ examples.

features.name: JUnit checkout evidence batch one
features.audience: Consultants validating JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 modernization evidence
features.summary: Promote Spring Petclinic REST JUnit and AssertJ Examples JUnit 4 from catalog-backed examples to checkout-backed validation evidence.
evidence: Promotes Spring Petclinic REST JUnit to checkout-backed evidence at git revision `517a399cabe4dd7a40c07a81facd599df3cbf9cd`.
Adds passing Maven compile and test validation evidence with 95% validation confidence.

Promotes AssertJ Examples JUnit 4 to checkout-backed module evidence at git revision `301fc0360dfe3cfbacb2fe7e63d4e32fb01798a0`, with passing Maven compilation and honest Neo4j-related test failure evidence.
Raises checkout-backed evidence from 30 to 32 reports and passing validation evidence from 13 to 14 reports while preserving the 75-report public catalog.

releases.title: v0.2.0
releases.summary: JUnit 5 readiness pack, 75-report catalog, complete Spring Security checkout evidence, and pack-specific Migration Hub pages.

features.name: Spring Security checkout completion
features.audience: Consultants validating Spring Security 6 readiness evidence
features.summary: Promote the remaining Spring Security readiness reports to checkout-backed evidence.
evidence: Promotes JHipster Sample Security to checkout-backed evidence with honest Maven validation failure evidence.
Promotes Keycloak Security Adapter to checkout-backed evidence with honest Maven compile failure and test timeout evidence.
Raises checkout-backed evidence from 28 to 30 reports while preserving 13 passing validation reports.

features.name: JUnit 5 readiness pack
features.audience: Enterprise Java teams migrating JUnit 4 test suites to JUnit 5
features.summary: Add a dedicated readiness pack for JUnit 4 to JUnit 5 modernization planning.
evidence: Adds `packs/junit-5-readiness.json`.
Detects JUnit 4 dependencies, imports, assertions, lifecycle annotations, runners, and Vintage engine usage.
Adds the testing readiness category and JUnit migration next actions.

features.name: JUnit benchmark evidence
features.audience: Consultants building public proof for test modernization work
features.summary: Add the first catalog-backed JUnit 5 readiness benchmark batch.
evidence: Adds 10 JUnit readiness reports.
Raises the public benchmark catalog from 65 to 75 reports.
Establishes the public report shape for test modernization evidence.

features.name: Pack-specific Migration Hub pages
features.audience: Consultants comparing evidence across modernization packs
features.summary: Generate a dedicated evidence hub page for every modernization pack.
evidence: Adds pack-specific Migration Hub pages for Spring Boot, Jakarta, Java LTS, Hibernate, Spring Security, and JUnit readiness.
Each hub summarizes total reports, checkout-backed reports, passing validation, failed validation, and report links.
The main Migration Hub now links to all pack-specific evidence hubs.

releases.title: v0.1.9
releases.summary: Spring Security checkout evidence batch with one passing compile/test validation report and two honest failure-evidence reports.

features.name: Spring Security checkout evidence batch one
features.audience: Consultants and enterprise Java teams validating Spring Security 6 readiness evidence
features.summary: Promote three Spring Security readiness reports from catalog-backed examples to checkout-backed evidence.
evidence: Promotes Spring Petclinic REST Security to checkout-backed evidence with passing Maven compile and test validation.
Promotes Spring Boot RealWorld Security to checkout-backed evidence with honest Gradle/JDK validation failure evidence.
Promotes Spring Security Samples Legacy to checkout-backed evidence with honest Gradle build failure evidence.
Raises checkout-backed evidence from 25 to 28 reports and passing validation evidence from 12 to 13 reports while preserving the 65-report public catalog.

releases.title: v0.1.8
releases.summary: Spring Security 6 readiness pack with security migration findings, Knowledge Base guidance, and first public benchmark evidence.

features.name: Spring Security 6 readiness pack
features.audience: Consultants and enterprise Java teams planning Spring Security 5.x to 6.x migrations
features.summary: Add a dedicated readiness pack for Spring Security 6 migration planning.
evidence: Adds `packs/spring-security-6-readiness.json`.
Detects Spring Security usage and explicit Spring Security 5.x versions when metadata is available.
Finds WebSecurityConfigurerAdapter, legacy matcher APIs, authorizeRequests configuration, and global method security annotations.
Adds report next actions for Spring Security 6 configuration risks.

features.name: Spring Security benchmark evidence
features.audience: Consultants building public proof for security modernization work
features.summary: Add the first catalog-backed Spring Security readiness benchmark batch.
evidence: Adds five Spring Security readiness reports.
Raises the public benchmark catalog from 60 to 65 reports.
Keeps checkout-backed evidence unchanged while establishing the public report shape for the new pack.

features.name: Spring Security 6 Knowledge Base
features.audience: Consultants explaining Spring Security migration risk
features.summary: Add Knowledge Base guidance for interpreting Spring Security 6 readiness findings.
evidence: Adds `knowledge/spring-security-6-readiness.json`.
Documents risks around removed adapters, matcher APIs, authorization DSL changes, method security, and Jakarta overlap.
Clarifies validation limits for security behavior.

releases.title: v0.1.7
releases.summary: Consultant Demo Pack for turning readiness evidence into a client-ready sales conversation.

features.name: Consultant Demo page
features.audience: Consultants using EMP in client conversations
features.summary: Add a static demo page that packages green-path, partial-validation, and advanced Hibernate evidence into a short client walkthrough.
evidence: Adds `docs/consultant-demo.html`.
Highlights Spring JavaConfig Sample, Java Hibernate Hello World, and Hypersistence Utils as the three recommended demo reports.
Includes a client conversation flow, commands to run, artifacts to send, and commercial next steps.

features.name: Consultant demo bundle
features.audience: Consultants preparing reusable client assets
features.summary: Add a repeatable generator for a downloadable consultant demo bundle containing selected reports, Knowledge Base pages, release notes, and a README.
evidence: Adds `npm run consultant:demo`.
Generates `reports/emp-consultant-demo.zip`.
Wires Consultant Demo generation into `npm run benchmarks:publish`.

releases.title: v0.1.6
releases.summary: Hibernate evidence expansion with five more checkout-backed reports and a validation failure Knowledge Base article.

features.name: Hibernate checkout evidence batch two
features.audience: Consultants and persistence modernization reviewers
features.summary: Add five more Hibernate readiness benchmarks and promote all five to checkout-backed validation evidence.
evidence: Adds Spring JavaConfig Sample, Spring 4 Hibernate 5 Example, Hibernate Spatial 5 Sample, Java Hibernate Hello World, and Bookstore Spring Hibernate.
Records passing compile and test validation for four of the five new reports.
Records compile-pass and test-failure evidence for Java Hibernate Hello World.
Raises the public catalog to 60 reports, checkout-backed evidence to 25 reports, and passing validation evidence to 12 reports.

features.name: Hibernate validation failure Knowledge Base
features.audience: Consultants explaining failed or partial readiness evidence
features.summary: Add a Knowledge Base article that explains how to interpret Java toolchain mismatches, missing wrappers, test dependency gaps, external service assumptions, and timeouts.
evidence: Adds `knowledge/hibernate-validation-failures.json`.
Publishes the generated `Hibernate Validation Failure Patterns` page.
Uses checkout-backed benchmark outcomes to distinguish environment blockers from Hibernate API risk.

releases.title: v0.1.5
releases.summary: Hibernate evidence expansion with additional checkout-backed validation outcomes.

features.name: Hibernate checkout validation evidence batch
features.audience: Consultants and persistence modernization reviewers
features.summary: Promote three more Hibernate readiness benchmarks from catalog-only examples to checkout-backed validation evidence with honest pass, failure, and timeout results.
evidence: Promotes Hypersistence Utils to checkout-backed Hibernate readiness evidence with Maven toolchain failure evidence for the required JDK 8 toolchain.
Promotes High Performance Java Persistence to checkout-backed Hibernate readiness evidence with validation timeout evidence under the current 120-second check budget.
Promotes Hibernate Test Case Templates to checkout-backed Hibernate readiness evidence with passing Maven compile validation and Docker-dependent reactive test failure evidence.
Raises the public evidence set to 20 checkout-backed reports while preserving 55 total benchmark reports.

releases.title: v0.1.4
releases.summary: Hibernate evidence upgrade with checkout-backed compile and test validation for Hibernate ORM Demos.

features.name: Hibernate ORM Demos checkout validation
features.audience: Consultants and persistence modernization reviewers
features.summary: Promote Hibernate ORM Demos from catalog-only Hibernate readiness evidence to checkout-backed evidence with passing Maven-wrapper compile and test validation.
evidence: Analyzes `hibernate/hibernate-demos` from the `hibernate-search/hsearch-with-elasticsearch` checkout subdirectory.
Publishes checkout-backed Hibernate readiness report evidence with readiness `83%`.
Records compile and test validation as `passed` with validation confidence `95%`.
Raises the public evidence set to 17 checkout-backed reports and 8 reports with passing validation.

releases.title: v0.1.3
releases.summary: Content engine improvements plus the first Month 7 mandatory pack expansion for Hibernate readiness.

features.name: Trust Engine confidence factors
features.audience: Consultants and Professional-tier reviewers
features.summary: Add explicit Trust Engine factors that explain confidence changes with status, impact, reason, and evidence.
evidence: Adds `trust.factors[]` to Professional trust JSON.
Adds a `Trust Factors` table to HTML reports.
Explains compile, test, rollback, compatibility, benchmark source, and validation confidence contributions.

features.name: Report next actions
features.audience: Consultants and client reviewers
features.summary: Add prioritized recommended next actions to readiness report JSON and HTML so reports move from diagnostics to client-actionable guidance.
evidence: Adds `report.nextActions` to generated JSON reports.
Adds a `Recommended Next Actions` section to HTML reports.
Derives actions from pack applicability, finding types, Hibernate risks, Jakarta readiness, Java target readiness, build metadata, and enterprise rules.

features.name: Hibernate readiness pack
features.audience: Java consultants and persistence modernization teams
features.summary: Add a Hibernate 5.x to 6.x readiness pack with ORM usage detection, pack applicability, static risk findings, readiness scoring, generated docs, and CLI visibility.
evidence: Adds `packs/hibernate-readiness.json`.
Detects Hibernate ORM usage from dependencies, source imports, and XML mappings.
Flags legacy Criteria API, direct Session API usage, custom type integrations, Hibernate XML mappings, and overlapping Jakarta namespace work.

features.name: Hibernate benchmark catalog batch
features.audience: Consultants and buyers reviewing persistence modernization evidence
features.summary: Add the first catalog-backed Hibernate readiness benchmark batch so the new pack has public evidence examples immediately.
evidence: Adds 5 Hibernate readiness benchmark reports.
Covers Hibernate demos, Hibernate test templates, Hypersistence Utils, High Performance Java Persistence, and Baeldung Hibernate tutorial material.
Raises the public benchmark catalog from 50 to 55 reports.

features.name: Hibernate Knowledge Base
features.audience: Consultants and enterprise modernization reviewers
features.summary: Generate a Knowledge Base section from structured guidance, starting with Hibernate readiness risks, validation notes, benchmark examples, and report limits.
evidence: Adds `knowledge/hibernate-readiness.json`.
Adds `npm run knowledge:generate`.
Publishes static Knowledge Base pages under `docs/knowledge-base/`.

features.name: Pack documentation generation
features.audience: Consultants and technical evaluators
features.summary: Generate static pack documentation from `packs/*.json`, including supported checks, readiness categories, report sections, and execution commands.
evidence: Adds `npm run docs:generate`.
Publishes static pages under `docs/packs/`.
Wires pack docs into the benchmark publishing content workflow.

features.name: Release notes from feature metadata
features.audience: Maintainers and release operators
features.summary: Generate public release-note pages and a GitHub-ready Markdown draft from structured feature metadata.
evidence: Adds `npm run release-notes:generate`.
Publishes release-note pages under `docs/release-notes/`.
Includes the generated Markdown draft in the same static output directory.

features.name: Repeatable benchmark publishing
features.audience: Maintainers and buyers reviewing public proof
features.summary: Regenerate the Migration Hub from checked-in benchmark evidence, enforce the benchmark count gate, and preserve checkout-backed validation evidence.
evidence: Adds `npm run benchmarks:publish`.
Verifies the current benchmark gate with `--min-count 55`.
Writes `reports/benchmark-publish-summary.json` during publication.

features.name: Expanded checkout-backed benchmark evidence
features.audience: Consultants and enterprise reviewers
features.summary: Promote additional public repositories from catalog-only reports to checkout-backed evidence, including honest pass, failure, timeout, and applicability results.
evidence: Public catalog contains 55 benchmark reports.
16 reports are checkout-backed.
7 checkout benchmarks currently pass compile and test validation.
